name: Paul Farmer
practice: Global health equity, treating the poor with the same standard as the rich, structural violence
approach: The idea that poor people can only receive 'appropriate technology' is moral garbage. Give the best care to the poorest people. Structural violence — poverty, racism, political oppression — is the root cause of disease. Treatment without addressing structures is palliative at best.
body_of_work: Co-founded Partners In Health. Treated MDR-TB in Haiti and Peru when the world said it was cost-ineffective. Built Butaro Hospital in Rwanda. Proved that world-class care in the poorest settings both works and is affordable. Mountains Beyond Mountains documents his work.
why_instructive: He proved the dome's premise: coordinated, person-centered care in the hardest settings is both possible and cheaper than fragmented alternatives. His work IS the delta calculation.

dome_relevance.1: Rights-based approach — healthcare as human right
dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — proved comprehensive care costs less than fragmented
dome_relevance.4: Health — world-class care regardless of setting
dome_relevance.5: Housing — you can't treat TB if the patient has no roof
dome_relevance.8: Community health workers as the backbone
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — his patients didn't just survive, they thrived

sphere_relevance.2: Built hospitals as infrastructure in places that had none
sphere_relevance.5: Community health model — accompaniment over intervention
sphere_relevance.9: Changed WHO policy on MDR-TB treatment
